title: Packit: Enabling the ppc64le and s390x builds to the used Copr
body:
### Feature request description

A clear and concise description of what the feature request is about.

When I was researching how to use Packit effectively in my involved project, I found this podman project using Packit with Copr practically. And I noticed that the Copr used for this project doesn't have the ppc64le and s390x cases, with the TODO document. However it seems all the ppc64le and s390x builds written in the text below are available in Copr now in the Settings - Build options. So, I just let you know that you can add these build options.

> https://copr.fedorainfracloud.org/coprs/rhcontainerbot/packit-builds/

> TODO: These envs take longer to build, so they have been disabled for now.
> * centos-stream-8-ppc64le
> * centos-stream-9-ppc64le
> * centos-stream-9-s390x
> * fedora-37-ppc64le
> * fedora-37-s390x
> * fedora-38-ppc64le
> * fedora-38-s390x
> * fedora-rawhide-ppc64le
> * fedora-rawhide-s390x


### Suggest potential solution

Add ppc64le and s390x to the Copr used by Packit.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

Yes. I considered the "upstream_koji_build" as an alternative. However, in the containers (podman)'s case, I believe that just adding the ppc64le and s390x builds to the Copr is the best.

https://packit.dev/docs/configuration/#upstream_koji_build
https://github.com/junaruga/ci-multi-arch-native-test/blob/21b6af5dd48a7fc455bc2f92c61e4f90af95d028/.packit.yml#L30-L39


### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body:
> TODO: These envs take longer to build, so they have been disabled for now.

I just noticed the reason you disabled the ppc64le and s390x was because the envs take longer to build rather than the envs not available. Sorry for my misunderstanding. Feel free to close this ticket.

Hey @junaruga , yes for the `trigger: pull_request` task we have disabled them as we noticed them taking upwards of an hour (sometimes stuck for like 8 hours). I've let folks in the packit team know that we'd really like speedups on those envs.

Anyway, RE: packit copr tasks, we have two of those. First runs on `trigger: pull_request` which only has x86_64 and aarch64 as you noticed, while the other `trigger: commit` task runs after upstream commit and doesn't consume any upstream time. The latter builds are sent to a different copr `rhcontainerbot/podman-next` and we have all fedora arches enabled there. Example: https://copr.fedorainfracloud.org/coprs/rhcontainerbot/podman-next/build/6005608/

Closing this, but feel free to respond if you've noticed any changes with ppc64le and s390x builds in recent times.
